review_comment: @davidfowl @halter73 and @pranavkm , I think this test is not needed anymore since we are adding the default content type (```application/json```) as ```IAcceptsMetadata``` when we call the ```RequestDelegate``` Create method. Any thoughts ?
Why isn't this required? Shouldn't we test it anyways?
I think this and the other from-body coverage we lost can be re-added with tests using TestEndpointRouteBuilder.
Aren't those covered with the metadata tests in ```EndpointMetadataApiDescriptionProviderTest.cs``` ? What will TestEndpointRouteBuilder test ?

I assume you mean DelegateEndpointRouteBuilderExtensionsTest? That should continue testing that the correct `IAcceptsMetadata` gets added.

We still need to test that the `IAcceptsMetadata` gets used and properly turned into the proper `SupportedRequestFormats` and `ParameterDescriptions`.

See the following test ```MapPost_BuildsEndpointWithCorrectEndpointMetadata``` in ```DelegateEndpointRouteBuilderExtensionsTest```.  It's exactly checking that we have the correct metadata. I can add an assertion to check if the type isRequired.  Anything else ? or Can you point me to an additional test class ?
That doesn't test anything about the `ApiDescription` that gets generated though.
I'll admit this I'm asking for more of an integration test, but we should have those and they already exist in this test class.
I added some more end to end tests.
